description: In a crowded coffee shop, Joey, Chandler, and their friends walk through the front door. Chandler points toward Joey and says, "You."
Joey turns to Chandler, saying, "I'm telling you, that girl totally winked at me." Chandler responds, "Did not wink at you." Several people talk over each other in the background.
The group stops walking and looks toward the center of the coffee shop. Overlapping voices say, "you're."
In the central seating area, six unfamiliar people are seated on the couches and chairs. The group stands still, staring ahead silently.
Chandler says, "Huh," while the rest of the group remains motionless, their gaze fixed forward.
Slowly and in unison, the group turns around and begins walking back toward the front door.
The screen fades to black, remaining dark for several seconds.
An exterior view shows a tall apartment building against a blue sky.
The setting changes to an apartment kitchen, where Phoebe, Ross, and Rachel sit around a small table eating.
Ross says, "Morrison." Rachel gestures with a fork and replies, "No way. Most romantic song ever was The Way We Were."
Phoebe gestures with both hands and says, "See, I think the one that Elton John wrote for"
Phoebe continues, "that guy on who's the Boss?"
Ross looks steadily at Phoebe, while Rachel lowers her fork.
Rachel looks at Phoebe and asks, "What song is that? Phoebes." Phoebe gestures with her hands and sings, "hold me close."
Phoebe continues singing, "Young Tony down."
Ross stares silently at Phoebe. Rachel puts a piece of food into her mouth.
Monica walks into the kitchen wearing a brown robe, her head lowered and her gaze directed at the floor.
Monica stops walking and says softly, "Hi." The group turns toward her, and overlapping voices say, "Monica. Hey, mom. Hey, mom."
Monica walks past the table toward the sink. Ross stands up and watches her.
Ross watches Monica and says, "Oh, my God."
Ross looks down at Phoebe and asks, "Has she slept at?" Phoebe shakes her head and replies, "No, no, it's been three nights in a row."

Ross picks up a coffee pot and pours coffee. Phoebe points toward the right side of the room and says, "Yeah, she finally stopped crying yesterday. But then she found one of Richard's cigar butts out on the terrace."
Phoebe lowers her hand.
Phoebe raises both hands in a grasping gesture and says, "Okay, that explains it. I got a call at two in the morning and all I could hear was, like, this high squeaky sound."
Lowering her hands, she continues, "So I thought, oh, okay,"
Phoebe raises her hands outward and says, "so it's like a mouse or a possum."
Phoebe lowers her hands.
Phoebe brings both hands to her chest, fingers interlaced, and says, "Then I realized, like, okay,"
Still holding her hands on her chest, she asks, "where would a mouse or a possum get the money to make the phone call?"
Ross sits silently, staring at Phoebe. The setting changes to a different apartment's living area as Joey walks out of a doorway.
Joey walks into the living area and says, "Morning." Chandler, standing by the kitchen counter, replies, "Hey." Joey spots a plate of food and asks, "You made pancakes?"
Chandler turns his head slightly toward Joey.
Chandler holds a newspaper, points toward the pancakes, and says, "Yeah, like there's any way I could ever do that."
The front door opens, and Janice enters carrying a bottle of syrup.
Janice walks toward Chandler, holding up the bottle, and sings, "Monica and Rachel had syrup."
She continues walking toward him, bouncing her shoulders, and sings, "Now I can get my man to cheer up," her voice rising and falling in pitch.
Chandler smiles at Janice as she laughs loudly.
Janice walks over to Joey, touches his chin lightly, and says, "Good morning, Joey." Joey looks down and replies quietly, "Good morning."
Janice points at Joey, steps back, and says loudly, "OOH, someone's wearing grumpy pants."
Janice keeps her arm extended toward Joey.
Chandler points at Joey and says, "Gotta be you, man."
Spreading his arms slightly, Chandler adds, "I'm wearing smarty pants."
Joey lowers his head, resting his hand on the counter.
Janice picks up a plate, walks back to Chandler, and uses a spatula to serve him, saying, "Okay, here we go. One, two"
She holds the spatula in front of him and sings, "and here's my chance to give you a third."
Chandler smiles, looking at the plate in his hand. Joey watches them from his seat.
Joey waves his hand slightly and says, "No, no. I'll just have juice."
Joey reaches for a juice carton on the counter and pours it into a glass.
Janice steps closer to Chandler, places her hands on his chest, and says, "Hey, you know what? Here's the thought. Why don't you stay home from work today and just hang out with me?"
Chandler steps back, putting the plate down, as Janice begins taking off her jacket. She says, "Oh, I wish. Listen, honey, you have that report to finish"
Janice wraps her arms around his waist and says, "and I've got to go see my lawyer. I cannot believe I'm going"
Holding onto Chandler, Janice continues, "out with somebody who's getting divorced. I'm such a grown up."
Janice smiles widely and looks up at Chandler.
Chandler turns his head toward Joey, points at him, and says, "Joey hey, go to your room."
Joey turns his head toward Chandler.
Joey looks at Chandler and says, "Chandler hey. Up yours."
Chandler turns back to Janice, adjusting his stance slightly.
Janice rests her hand on his chest and says rapidly, "I gotta go. I gotta go."
Janice asks, "Okay? Not without a kiss." Chandler replies, "Well, maybe I won't kiss you"
Chandler continues, "and then you'll have to stay."
Joey, sitting at the counter, says firmly, "Kiss her. Kiss her."
Chandler and Janice lean toward each other and kiss on the lips.
Janice pulls away, grabs her bag, and says, "I'll see you later, sweetie. Bye, Joey." Joey says, "Bye." Chandler adds, "Bye, Janice," as she walks out the door.
In an apartment kitchen, Joey sits at a small counter holding a glass as a door clicks shut. No dialogue is spoken.

Chandler turns from the door, adjusting his tie. Joey asks, "So when are you dumping her?" Chandler walks toward the kitchen counter and replies, "Nope, not this time," drawing audience laughter. Joey gestures with his hands and says, "Come on, quit yanking me."
Chandler pours coffee from a pot into a green mug while standing at the counter. Joey watches him.
Chandler stops pouring, looks at Joey, and says, "I'm not yanking you."
Chandler lowers the coffee pot as the audience laughs.
Joey points his hand toward the door and says, "This is Janice."
Chandler holds his mug and says, "Yeah, I know. She makes me happy."
Chandler walks toward Joey.
Joey says, "Okay."
Joey adds, "All right."
Joey points his finger at Chandler and says, "You look me in the eye and tell me without blinking that you're not breaking up with her. No blinking." Chandler stares back, drawing audience laughter.
Chandler maintains an unblinking stare at Joey.
Chandler says, "I'm not breaking up with her," keeping his eyes wide open.
Joey leans his face closer to Chandler's, scrutinizing him. Chandler continues to stare without blinking as the audience laughs.

Chandler suddenly thrusts his hands toward Joey's face, making Joey flinch and blink while the audience laughs loudly. The setting changes to a different apartment where Ross, Rachel, and Phoebe sit at a round table. Monica enters wearing a brown robe.
Monica walks toward the table, holding her hands together, and says, "Look what I found in the drain."
Monica stands near the table, looking down at a small dark item in her hands.
Monica says, "It's some of Richard's hair."
Ross looks up from his cereal bowl toward Monica as audience laughter is heard.
Monica holds the hair up and asks, "What do I do with this?" Ross replies, "Getting it away from me would be job one."
Monica lowers the hair slightly. Audience laughter plays.
Monica holds the hair in both hands and says, "It's weird, but you know what? I don't want to throw this away. I mean, this is like, all I have left of him. Gross"
Monica shakes her hands abruptly, dropping the hair, and says, "drain hair. Oh," drawing audience laughter.
Monica steps back, clutching her head. The hair falls toward Ross, who stares down into his cereal bowl with a grimace.
Phoebe stands up and leans over Ross's shoulder, looking into his bowl.
Phoebe points into the bowl and says, "Oh, it looks like like a tiny little person drowning in your cereal," accompanied by audience laughter.
Ross pushes his chair back, picks up his bowl, and stands up. Monica touches her forehead with her hand.
Monica asks, "God, what is wrong with me?" Ross, carrying his bowl to the sink, replies, "You need to get some sleep." Monica adds, "I need to get some Richard," followed by audience laughter.
Monica turns and walks over to the refrigerator.
Rachel says, "Monica, you broke up with him for a reason." Monica walks back to the table and replies, "I know. I know. Just so tired of missing him. I'm" She pours orange juice from a pitcher.
Monica sets down the pitcher and says, "tired of wondering why he hasn't called. Why hasn't he called?"
Phoebe, stirring her mug, says, "Maybe because you told him not to." Monica points at her and replies, "What are you, the Memory Woman?" as the audience laughs.
Joey enters the apartment rapidly, gesturing with his hands.
Joey points and says, "They're not breaking up. Chandler and Janice, they're not breaking up."
Joey gestures further and says, "He didn't blink or anything," drawing laughter.
Rachel continues eating her breakfast.
Rachel says, "Well, you know, I'm not surprised. I mean, have you seen them together? They're really cute."
Joey waves his hands and says, "Cute? This is Janice. You remember Janice."

Rachel points her fork and says, "Yes, Joey. I remember. She's annoying. But you know what? She's his girlfriend now. I mean, what can we do?" Joey points at her and adds, "There you go," over audience laughter.
Joey gestures broadly and says, "That's the spirit I'm looking for. What can we?"
Joey looks around the room and turns toward Ross.
Joey claps his hands and asks, "All right, who's first, huh? Ross?"
Ross turns around from the sink area.
Ross says, "Well, I'm thinking Chandler's our friend and Janice makes him happy. So I say we all just be adult about it and accept"
Ross finishes, "her." Joey lowers his arms, points toward Ross, and says to the group, "Yeah, we'll call that plan B. All right," followed by audience laughter.
Joey turns back to the table, looking at the others.
Joey gestures to the group and says, "come on, people. We need some inspiration." Phoebe raises her hands and says, "Yeah, okay. All right. You want a plan? Here we go."
Phoebe says, "All right."
Phoebe points at Ross and says, "Okay. Ross, you go into the air duct and disable the alarm, okay?"
Phoebe brings her hands up to her chest as laughter is heard.
Phoebe mimics pulling a rope downward and says, "The rest of us will just rappel down ropes. We're in and out in ten minutes and disappear into the night." The audience laughs loudly.
Joey, Monica, and Rachel stare silently at Phoebe as audience laughter continues.
The setting changes to an exterior night view of a tall apartment building.
The setting changes to a dimly lit bedroom. Ross and Rachel sit up in bed under a striped blanket. Ross writes on a yellow notepad while Rachel holds a magazine.
Rachel turns her head, leans over, and rests her cheek against Ross's shoulder.
Ross continues writing on the notepad while Rachel rests against his shoulder, keeping her eyes open.

In a dimly lit bedroom, Ross sits up in bed holding a notepad and pen. Rachel sits beside him, resting her arm around his shoulders and leaning her head against him. She lifts her head to look at him.
Rachel asks, "I was wondering, do you still have that Navy uniform?" Ross turns his head to meet her gaze.
Rachel smiles as she continues looking at Ross. He lowers his gaze slightly.
Ross looks back up at her and says, "No, I had to return it to the costume place."
Ross adds, "I think I have an old band uniform from high school," as audience laughter is heard.
Rachel maintains her gaze on him.
Rachel says, "You remember not having sex in high school, right?"
Ross replies, "Yeah." Audience laughter follows.
Ross looks down at his notepad. Rachel withdraws her arm from his shoulder.
Rachel leans her head back and asks, "Honey, what about you?"
Rachel asks, "Any fun, you know, fantasy type things?" Ross keeps his gaze lowered before briefly looking at her.
Ross shakes his head slightly and says, "No."
Rachel says, "Come on, you gotta have one." Ross shakes his head again, replying, "Nope."
Rachel leans forward slightly, looking at him, and says, "Ross."
Rachel says, "You know what? If you tell me, I might do it."
Ross looks down at his notepad, a slight smile forming on his face. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross turns to look at Rachel, tossing his notepad onto the bed.
Ross says, "Okay."
Ross shifts his body to face her more directly, moving his hands.
Ross asks, "Did you ever see Return of the Jedi?"
Rachel keeps her gaze on him, her expression unchanged.
Rachel continues to look at him in silence as audience laughter plays.
Rachel replies, "Yeah."
Ross says, "Do you remember the scene with"
Ross pauses, his eyes shifting away briefly.
Ross finishes, "Jabba the Hut?"
Rachel frowns slightly, her brow furrowing.
Rachel says, "He was this really fat worm guy."
Ross opens his mouth, his gaze dropping momentarily.
Rachel says, "Wow, men and women really are"
Ross raises both hands with palms facing forward.
Ross interrupts, saying, "no, no."
Ross says, "See, Jabba had as his prisoner Princess Leia"
Rachel's lips part into a smile as audience laughter is heard.
Ross gestures with one hand and says, "because Princess Leia was wearing this"
Ross holds his hands apart, tracing a shape in the air.
Ross continues, "gold bikini thing."
Ross nods slightly, keeping his gaze on her as audience laughter continues.
Ross adds, "It was pretty cool."
Rachel places her hand on his shoulder and says, "So are you saying that you would like me to"
Rachel slides her hand up his neck, moving closer to him.
Rachel leans in, kissing his neck, and finishes, "dress up like Princess Leia in a gold bikini?" Ross throws his arms up and says, "Okay." Audience laughter and cheers are heard.
The setting changes to a coffee shop. Phoebe sits on a couch holding a blue mug, with Rachel seated next to her.
Phoebe gestures with her free hand and says, "Yeah. Oh, princess Leia in the gold bikini. Every guy our age loved that."
Rachel holds a crumpled napkin, looking at Phoebe.
Rachel asks, "Really?"
Phoebe nods multiple times.
Phoebe says, "It's huge. Yeah, that's the moment when she stopped being a princess and she became, like, a woman."
Rachel holds her hands together in her lap, looking at Phoebe.
Rachel asks, "Did you ever do the Leia thing?"
Phoebe smiles, raising her eyebrows.
Phoebe says, "Oh, yeah."
Rachel smiles broadly and lets out a small laugh.
Rachel says, "Really? That's great." Phoebe then shifts suddenly on the couch, her body vibrating slightly.
Reaching into her open bag beside her, Phoebe says, "No, it's just that I got this new pager and I have it on vibrate."
Phoebe pulls a small black pager from her bag and looks down at it.
Phoebe stands up and walks away from the couch. Rachel watches her go.
Ross walks into the coffee shop and says, "Hey."
Chandler follows Ross inside and says, "Hi, you guys. Look who I found standing outside the Sichuan dragon staring at a parking meter."
Ross stands next to Chandler with his gaze slightly lowered.
Inside the coffee shop, Ross guides Monica forward by her shoulders. Rachel stands nearby and turns to face them.
Rachel says, "Mon."
Monica says, "Hi," keeping her gaze lowered.
Rachel looks closely at Monica's face.
"Why aren't you at work?" Rachel asks.
"Oh, they sent me home," Monica replies.
"Why?" Rachel asks.
Monica pauses, continuing to look downward.
"Because I don't work at the Sichuan dragon. Okay," Monica says.
Ross steps forward, guiding Monica toward a nearby couch.
Monica sits down and says, "I really need to get some sleep."
Ross leans toward her and says, "I know I do."
Ross turns to Rachel. He says, "Hi," and she replies, "Hi." He asks, "Guess what?" She asks, "What?" "They published my paper," he tells her. Rachel smiles and asks, "Oh, really?" "Yeah," he says.
"Statistical deviations in the carbon dating of fossilized Algae," Ross says, gesturing with his hands.
"Calling history or mystery?" Ross adds as Rachel's smile fades.
Rachel nods slowly while Ross lowers his head. Phoebe approaches from behind the couch, holding a large bread roll over each ear.
"Rach, look," Phoebe says.
Rachel turns and covers her mouth, laughing.
"Hi," Phoebe says with a smile.
Rachel touches Phoebe's arm, continuing to laugh. Ross watches them, his facial features remaining still.
Holding the bread to her head, Phoebe says, "Where is my strong rock skywalker to come rescue me?"
Ross maintains a fixed gaze on Phoebe.
Rachel glances back and forth between Phoebe and Ross.
Phoebe points one of the rolls at Ross and says, "There he is." Ross's expression remains unchanged.

The setting changes to Chandler and Joey's apartment. On a television screen, a game show host stands next to a puzzle reading "O U N  R U S H  O R E" above the word "LANDMARK". Chandler walks through the front door as Joey sits in a recliner facing the screen.
Chandler says, "Hey." A voice from the television says, "Wheel of Fortune."
Joey gestures toward the television.
Joey raises his hands and says, "This guy's so stupid, it's Count Rushmore." Chandler stops and stares at him.
Chandler stands still, his eyes wide as he looks at Joey.
"You know, you should really go on this show," Chandler says.
Chandler pulls tickets from his jacket pocket.
Chandler extends a ticket toward Joey and says, "All right, listen. I got three tickets to the Rangers tonight."
"What do you say?" Chandler asks.
Joey takes the ticket.
"I say I am there," Joey says. "Cool," Chandler replies.
Joey looks at the ticket and asks, "Oh, is Ross going too?" Chandler replies, "No, janice."
Joey lowers the ticket, his jaw dropping slightly.
"Janice," Joey repeats.
Chandler nods his head.
Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "Because I feel bad. I mean, we always go together."
"We're like the three hockey" Chandler adds.
Joey stands up from the recliner.
Chandler points at Joey and asks, "You know, I may be way out on a limb here, but do you have a problem with Janice?"
Joey replies, "Oh,"
Joey gestures with one hand.
"yes," Joey says.
Chandler places a hand on his hip.
"How do I say this?" Joey asks as he walks past Chandler.
Joey turns back to face Chandler.
"Oh, duh," Joey says, snapping his fingers.
Joey gestures broadly with his arms.
"You know that girl from the Greek restaurant with the hair?" Joey asks, gesturing near his head. "OOH, that girl," Chandler replies.
"That hair. She drives me crazy. Oh," Joey adds.
Joey lowers his hands.
"look, I don't hate Janice. She's just a lot to know," Joey says.
Chandler stares at Joey.
"Well, there you go," Chandler says.
"Oh, hey, come on, man. Don't look at me like that. She used to drive you nuts before too, remember?" Joey says, pointing at Chandler.
"Yeah, well, I'm crazy about her now," Chandler says.
"I think this could be the real thing. Capital R, capital T," Chandler adds, tapping his chest.
Joey places his hands near his waist.
"Don't worry, those are the right letters," Joey says.
Chandler turns slightly away.
"Look," Joey says, walking toward the kitchen area.
Chandler turns back to face him.
Joey stands by a foosball table and asks, "what do you want me to say?"
Chandler steps toward Joey and says, "I want you to say that you like her." Joey leans back and replies, "I can't like her."
Chandler steps closer.
"So you're not even going to try?" Chandler asks.
Joey throws his hands out and says, "What's the point? It's like this chemical thing."
"Every time she starts laughing, I just want to" Joey says.
Joey mimics pulling on his arm.
Joey yanks his arm forward and says, "pull my arm off just so I have something to throw at her."
Chandler stares at Joey without speaking.
"Thanks for trying," Chandler says, turning toward the door.
Chandler reaches the door and grabs the knob.
Chandler stops, looks back, and says, "Oh, and by the way, there is no Count Rushmore."
Joey tilts his head.
Joey points at Chandler and says, "Yeah? Then who's the guy that painted the faces on the mountain?"
Chandler stares silently at Joey.
Chandler maintains his silent gaze on Joey until the scene concludes.
In an apartment kitchen, Ross stands wearing a suit and a shoulder bag. Rachel stands with her back to him, wearing a sleeveless patterned dress. Ross asks, "How could you have told her?"
Rachel turns around holding a small black item, walks toward the center of the kitchen, and says, "Ross, I didn't think it would be that big a deal."
Ross looks away from Rachel, gestures with one hand, and says, "Oh, she didn't think it would be that big a." He removes his bag from his shoulder and begins setting it on a chair.
Ross says, "deal?" as he finishes placing the bag down. Rachel raises both hands with open palms and asks, "Okay, who are you talking to when you do that?" Audience laughter begins.
Ross shifts his gaze back to Rachel as the audience laughter continues.
Ross gestures back and forth between himself and Rachel, saying, "Look, that was supposed to be like a private, personal thing between us, okay?"
Rachel places both hands against her upper chest and says, "Ross, Phoebe is my girlfriend. Okay? We tell each other." At 00:18, the screen turns black and the audio stops.
The screen remains black and silent.
The silent black screen continues.
